text.title: Refactor Baseline Metrics Script Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the refactor-baseline-metrics-script methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Script uses Python stdlib only. No external pip dependencies.
rule.rationale: External deps drift between checkouts; stdlib-only stays portable.

rule.statement: Running on the same SHA twice produces byte-identical output.
Output contains at least LOC, cyclomatic complexity, hotspot churn (commits in last 90d), test runtime sec, coverage pct.
rule.rationale: Fewer metrics misses regressions; more becomes noise; 5 is the floor.

rule.statement: Primary output is JSON to stdout (compactable). Markdown summary optional via --md flag.
rule.rationale: JSON is machine-readable for CI; markdown is for humans.

rule.statement: Script ships with --self-test running against an inline fixture; CI checks invariants.
rule.rationale: Self-test prevents script rot; we ship our own contract.

rule.statement: Hotspot window (default 90d) is configurable but consistent across compared branches.
rule.rationale: Different windows produce incomparable hotspot scores.

rule.statement: If change is a rename/reformat OR no base branch OR org tooling covers it, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Script ceremony is wasted in those configurations.
